chapter_title.ar: باب صَدَقَاتِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ فَاطِمَةَ وَ الْأَئِمَّةِ ( عليهم السلام ) وَ وَصَايَاهُمْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 35 – The charities of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, and Fatima<sup>asws</sup>, and the Imams<sup>asws</sup>, and their<sup>asws</sup> bequests

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/91)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (3/91)
text: قَالَ إِنَّ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) مَاتَ وَ تَرَكَ سِتِّينَ غُلَاماً فَأَعْتَقَ ثُلُثَهُمْ فَأَقْرَعْتُ بَيْنَهُمْ فَأَخْرَجْتُ عِشْرِينَ فَأَعْتَقْتُهُمْ .

en.hubeali: Humayd Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Muhammad Bin Sama’at, from Ja’far Bin Sama’at, and someone else, from Aban, from Muhammad Bin Marwan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> passed away and left (as legacy) sixty slaves, so he<sup>asws</sup> emancipated (freed) a third of them. So I<sup>asws</sup> drew lots between them, so I<sup>asws</sup> brought out twenty and freed them’.
en.sarwar: 12. Flumayd ibn Ziyad has narrated from al-Hassan ibn Muhammad ibn Sama‘ah, from Ja‘far ibn Sama‘ah and from more than one person from Aban from Muhammad ibn Marwan who has narrated the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, died and left sixty slaves, of whom he set free one-third. I picked up the ones to be free by means of casting a raffle.’”
